title: Terminal restoration outside event loop on all exit paths
description:
GOOD CHOICE: Terminal raw mode and alternate screen are disabled AFTER the event loop exits (main.rs 177-178), even if the loop exits via error. The disable happens unconditionally outside the try block.

A contributor might put terminal restoration inside a finally-like block or only on success paths. But if error handling short-circuits before reaching the restore code, the terminal would be left in raw mode, causing corruption of the user's shell.

This ordering (try event loop, then always restore) is non-obvious but load-bearing for terminal safety.
close_reason: Verified invariant holds in current code; documented in DESIGN.md §14 TUI terminal safety invariants
